heading: Combatant commands: assigned forces; chain of command
label: 162 Combatant commands: assigned forces; chain of command
notes: Amendments2018—Subsec. (a)(2). substituted “section 7013, 8013, or 9013” for “section 3013, 5013, or 8013”.Pub. L. 115–2322017—Subsec. (a)(4). struck out comma after “command of”.Pub. L. 115–912016—Subsec. (a)(1). , substituted “As directed by the Secretary of Defense” for “Except as provided in paragraph (2)” and “specified forces” for “all forces” and struck out “Such assignments shall be made as directed by the Secretary of Defense, including direction as to the command to which forces are to be assigned.” before “The Secretary of Defense”.Pub. L. 114–328, § 924(1)Subsec. (a)(2). , added par. (2) and struck out former par. (2) which read as follows: “Except as otherwise directed by the Secretary of Defense, forces to be assigned by the Secretaries of the military departments to the combatant commands or to the United States element of the North American Aerospace Defense Command under paragraph (1) do not include forces assigned to carry out functions of the Secretary of a military department listed in sections 3013(b), 5013(b), and 8013(b) of this title or forces assigned to multinational peacekeeping organizations.”Pub. L. 114–328, § 924(2)Subsec. (a)(4). , struck out “assigned to, and” before “under the command”.Pub. L. 114–328, § 924(3)(B), which directed striking out “operating with the geographic area”, was executed by striking out “operating within the geographic area” after “all forces” to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 114–328, § 924(3)(A)1996—Subsec. (a)(1) to (3). substituted “North American Aerospace Defense Command” for “North American Air Defense Command”.Pub. L. 104–2011988—Subsec. (a)(1) to (3). inserted “or to the United States element of the North American Air Defense Command”.Pub. L. 100–4561987—Subsec. (a)(2). inserted before period at end “or forces assigned to multinational peacekeeping organizations”.Pub. L. 100–180

Implementation of Assignment of Forces to Combatant Commands, , , provided that shall be implemented not later than 90 days after .Pub. L. 99–433, title II, § 214(a)100 Stat. 1018section 162(a) of this titleOct. 1, 1986Oct. 1, 1986

children.text: As directed by the Secretary of Defense, the Secretaries of the military departments shall assign specified forces under their jurisdiction to unified and specified combatant commands or to the United States element of the North American Aerospace Defense Command to perform missions assigned to those commands. The Secretary of Defense shall ensure that such assignments are consistent with the force structure prescribed by the President for each combatant command.

A force not assigned to a combatant command or to the United States element of the North American Aerospace Defense Command under paragraph (1) shall remain assigned to the military department concerned for carrying out the responsibilities of the Secretary of the military department concerned as specified in section 7013, 8013, or 9013 of this title, as applicable.

children.label: A
children.text: by authority of the Secretary of Defense; and

children.label: B
children.text: under procedures prescribed by the Secretary and approved by the President.

Except as otherwise directed by the Secretary of Defense, all forces assigned to a unified combatant command shall be under the command of the commander of that command. The preceding sentence applies to forces assigned to a specified combatant command only as prescribed by the Secretary of Defense.
from the President to the Secretary of Defense; and
from the Secretary of Defense to the commander of the combatant command.
